description: A plain candid documentary photograph, not a designed graphic. It shows an outdoor parking-lot scene where an ICE officer in a dark blue raid jacket clearly marked 'POLICE' and 'ICE' (yellow lettering on the back) is loading or unloading a male detainee at the open side door of a large black SUV (an ICE transport vehicle, with a wire-mesh prisoner partition visible inside). The detainee, a dark-haired man in a black track jacket with white sleeve stripes, sits/steps in the doorway with his hands apparently restrained in front. The officer wears blue jeans. Background shows trees, a fence, and overcast sky. There is NO designed on-image text, no headline, slogan, or graphic overlay, and no added typography or seal beyond the agency markings naturally printed on the officer's jacket. The photo accompanies a tweet about ICE ERO Los Angeles turning a fugitive (wanted in Mexico for child rape) over to Mexican authorities at the San Ysidro port of entry. Intent is to document/publicize an enforcement transfer, but the image itself is an undesigned operational photo.
